# Tokio Compatibility Crate Scaffolding and Versioning Policy

**Bead**: `asupersync-2oh2u.7.3` ([T7.3])
**Generated by**: WhiteDesert (codex-cli / gpt-5-codex)
**Date**: 2026-03-03
**Version**: 1.0.0
**Dependencies**: T7.2 (adapter boundary architecture)
**Purpose**: Define explicit, testable policy for compatibility-crate scaffolding,
versioning, ownership, and release gating so Tokio interop code remains isolated,
auditable, and operationally reversible.

---

## 1. Scope and Intent

This policy governs `asupersync-tokio-compat` as the only sanctioned boundary
for Tokio-locked ecosystem interoperability.

Goals:
- Keep Tokio coupling out of core `asupersync` runtime paths.
- Keep adapter surface evolvable without destabilizing core runtime contracts.
- Ensure promotion and rollback decisions are objective and reproducible.
- Make ownership and escalation paths explicit for all exceptions.

Out of scope:
- Implementing full adapter runtime/task/time/io logic (owned by T7.4+).
- Defining third-party crate-specific conformance matrices (owned by T7.7+).

---

## 2. Scaffolding Model

### 2.1 Workspace and Dependency Topology

Required workspace topology:

```text
asupersync (core runtime)  ← no dependency edge from core to compat
asupersync-tokio-compat    → depends on asupersync (one-way)
```

Scaffolding invariants:
- Compatibility crate is a workspace member.
- Compatibility crate has `default = []` features.
- Core crate does not list `asupersync-tokio-compat` as a dependency.
- Tokio dependency in compat crate is constrained to trait-bridge needs.

### 2.2 Policy Metadata Anchors

`asupersync-tokio-compat/Cargo.toml` must include:
- `[package.metadata.asupersync_compat_policy]`
- `policy_version = "1.0.0"`
- `compatibility_line = "0.1.x"`
- `release_train = "tokio-compat"`
- `owner_track = "asupersync-2oh2u.7"`
- `exception_review = "threaded-agent-mail"`

`asupersync-tokio-compat/src/lib.rs` must expose:
- `COMPAT_POLICY_VERSION`
- `COMPATIBILITY_LINE`
- `OWNER_TRACK_ID`

These anchors provide stable parse points for automation, release tooling, and
human review.

---

## 3. Policy Statements and Enforcement Mapping

| Policy ID | Policy Statement | Enforcement Mechanism | Evidence Artifact |
|-----------|------------------|-----------------------|-------------------|
| `POL-T7.3-01` | Core runtime paths MUST remain Tokio-free. | Review gate + architecture checks from T7.2 contract tests. | `docs/tokio_adapter_boundary_architecture.md` + `tests/tokio_adapter_boundary_architecture.rs` |
| `POL-T7.3-02` | Compatibility layer MUST remain in dedicated crate (`asupersync-tokio-compat`). | Workspace membership + dependency topology review. | `Cargo.toml`, `asupersync-tokio-compat/Cargo.toml` |
| `POL-T7.3-03` | Compat crate feature defaults MUST be empty to prevent accidental activation. | Static Cargo policy check in contract tests. | `asupersync-tokio-compat/Cargo.toml`, `tests/tokio_compatibility_crate_scaffolding_policy.rs` |
| `POL-T7.3-04` | All public adapter entry points MUST remain explicit-capability oriented (no ambient authority). | API review + invariant contract tests. | `asupersync-tokio-compat/src/lib.rs`, T7.4+ adapter tests |
| `POL-T7.3-05` | No hidden Tokio runtime bootstrap in compat scaffolding. | Code review grep + clippy/test gate. | `asupersync-tokio-compat/src/**` |
| `POL-T7.3-06` | Policy/version ownership metadata MUST be present and consistent. | Cargo metadata + lib constants contract tests. | `asupersync-tokio-compat/Cargo.toml`, `asupersync-tokio-compat/src/lib.rs` |
| `POL-T7.3-07` | Unsafe code MUST remain denied in compat crate. | Rust lint gate (`unsafe_code = deny`). | `asupersync-tokio-compat/Cargo.toml` |
| `POL-T7.3-08` | Promotion to next interop bead MUST pass objective quality gates. | RCH validation command bundle and threshold checks. | Section 4 gate table + CI logs |
| `POL-T7.3-09` | Rollback decisions MUST preserve deterministic diagnostics and clear operator procedure. | Hard-fail triage + rollback matrix. | Section 4 rollback table |
| `POL-T7.3-10` | Exception requests MUST include owner approval and bounded TTL. | Agent Mail thread + exception template workflow. | Thread `asupersync-2oh2u.7.3` records |

---

## 4. Promotion and Rollback Criteria

### 4.1 Promotion Gates (Objective)

| Gate ID | Requirement | Threshold | Command / Check |
|---------|-------------|-----------|-----------------|
| `GATE-T7.3-PROM-01` | T7.3 policy contract tests pass | 100% pass | `rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_compatibility_crate_scaffolding_policy -- --nocapture` |
| `GATE-T7.3-PROM-02` | Prior architecture boundary contract remains valid | 100% pass | `rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_adapter_boundary_architecture -- --nocapture` |
| `GATE-T7.3-PROM-03` | Workspace and targets compile | exit code 0 | `rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets -q` |
| `GATE-T7.3-PROM-04` | Formatting compliance | exit code 0 | `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` |
| `GATE-T7.3-PROM-05` | Lint baseline and warnings enforcement | exit code 0 | `rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` |

Promotion to `T7.4` is blocked if any `GATE-T7.3-PROM-*` gate fails.

### 4.2 Rollback Triggers and Actions

| Trigger ID | Trigger Condition | Immediate Action | Resume Condition |
|------------|-------------------|------------------|------------------|
| `RB-T7.3-01` | Policy metadata missing or inconsistent | Revert to last passing policy snapshot, re-run `PROM-01` | Metadata restored and tests green |
| `RB-T7.3-02` | Core crate accidentally references compat crate | Reject change, remove dependency edge, run `PROM-03` | One-way topology restored |
| `RB-T7.3-03` | New default feature introduces hidden behavior | Disable default feature, force explicit opt-in, rerun checks | `default = []` restored |
| `RB-T7.3-04` | Lint/format/compile regressions from scaffolding changes | Roll back offending commit range and replay validation bundle | Full promotion gate set passes |
| `RB-T7.3-05` | Missing ownership/escalation decision for exception | Halt promotion and open exception thread with owner sign-off | Owner approval + expiry window recorded |

Rollback actions are operationally safe because policy artifacts are text and
metadata-first; no destructive history rewrites are required.

---

## 5. Ownership, Escalation, and Exceptions

### 5.1 Ownership Model

| Area | Primary Owner | Backup Owner | Escalation Thread |
|------|----------------|--------------|-------------------|
| Compatibility crate scaffolding | T7 assignee (`WhiteDesert` for current execution) | Any active T7 agent with reservation | `asupersync-2oh2u.7.3` |
| Versioning policy metadata | T7 assignee | Release/packaging maintainer | `asupersync-2oh2u.7.3` |
| Promotion/rollback gating | T7 assignee + reviewer agent | Project operator (`ubuntu`) | `asupersync-2oh2u.7` |

### 5.2 Exception Workflow

1. Open Agent Mail thread reply under `asupersync-2oh2u.7.3`.
2. Include affected `POL-T7.3-*` IDs and proposed temporary waiver.
3. Provide bounded validity window (TTL) and rollback plan.
4. Obtain explicit owner approval in-thread.
5. Attach validation output proving no invariant break.
6. Close exception with post-merge remediation or permanent policy update.

Exception approvals without policy IDs or TTL are invalid.

---

## 6. CI and Review Gate Integration

### 6.1 Mandatory Command Bundle (RCH)

```bash
rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_compatibility_crate_scaffolding_policy -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo test --test tokio_adapter_boundary_architecture -- --nocapture
rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets -q
rch exec -- cargo fmt --check
rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings
```

### 6.2 Review Checklist

- `POL-T7.3-01..10` all mapped to concrete artifacts.
- Cargo metadata policy block present and values unchanged unless version bump approved.
- `COMPAT_POLICY_VERSION`, `COMPATIBILITY_LINE`, and `OWNER_TRACK_ID` remain aligned with Cargo metadata.
- Promotion or rollback decision references explicit gate IDs.
- Agent Mail thread includes start, completion, and release notes.
